corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: غَنِمْتُم is a finite second-person plural perfect verb; review must account for person/number and context before any authored hover is certified
drill_prompt: Given غَنِمْتُم, name the finite-form fields missing from “to take war gains”.
learner_explanation: غَنِمْتُم contributes a plural completed action, not the dictionary label “to take war gains”.
nahw_lesson: Context chooses the object and wording, but it cannot erase that the token is finite and plural.
sarf_lesson: For finite verbs, record aspect, voice, person, number, gender, and form before accepting entry prose.
what_failed: A populated hover reused an infinitive war-gains entry instead of the exact finite token contribution.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: ٱقْذِفِيهِ must preserve the imperative/verb host plus the attached هِ object/location relation before a learner-safe hover can be reviewed
drill_prompt: Segment ٱقْذِفِيهِ into verb host and suffix, then state the next gate.
learner_explanation: The final هِ cannot disappear behind a bare “to throw” gloss.
nahw_lesson: The suffix relation and referent determine whether the wording is “it”, “him”, “therein”, or another contextual contribution.
sarf_lesson: Verb hosts with attached suffixes must keep the suffix in parse, display segments, and learner explanation.
what_failed: Entry-family prose hid the attached pronominal/locative suffix on a command token.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: فَأَهْلَكْنَاهُمْ contains fāʾ, a finite verb host, and هُمْ object; component evidence cannot certify the whole token
drill_prompt: Explain why فَأَهْلَكْنَاهُمْ remains component-only in this tranche.
learner_explanation: فَأَهْلَكْنَاهُمْ is not just “so We destroyed them” unless the whole token is certified by exact address and reason.
nahw_lesson: The fāʾ relation and object referent may affect learner wording and require compatible reasoning.
sarf_lesson: Component candidates must remain separate from whole-token candidates and cannot weaken the gate.
what_failed: A component candidate was useful for routing but not whole-token proof.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: هُمْ is a standalone pronoun/function row; a verb-entry family candidate must not certify or propagate it
drill_prompt: Classify هُمْ as pronoun/function evidence and explain why source-family propagation is unsafe.
learner_explanation: هُمْ may be readable as “they/their”, but it still needs exact pronoun role and public-safe segmentation before rich certification.
nahw_lesson: Standalone pronouns need nahw role/referent review, not a verb-entry gloss family.
sarf_lesson: Strict POS/function split blocks verb-family reuse on pronoun/function tokens.
what_failed: A verb-entry candidate collided with a standalone pronoun surface.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: لِفُرُوجِهِمْ requires lām relation, host noun, and هِمْ suffix before any whole-token hover is certified
drill_prompt: Segment لِفُرُوجِهِمْ and name why component evidence is not enough.
learner_explanation: لِفُرُوجِهِمْ is not just the host meaning; it includes “for/to” relation and “their”.
nahw_lesson: The lām relation and attachment determine the English contribution and require context review.
sarf_lesson: A lām-prefixed nominal host with suffix must preserve preposition, host, and suffix as separate pieces.
what_failed: Component evidence hid the preposition-host-suffix structure.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: مَغَانِمُ is a plural nominal row; do not certify it from the bare verb-family infinitive without POS/number/case review
drill_prompt: Explain why مَغَانِمُ blocks a verb-infinitive hover.
learner_explanation: مَغَانِمُ should be reviewed as a nominal plural contribution, not as “to take war gains”.
nahw_lesson: Case and phrase role decide the public wording; the entry family does not.
sarf_lesson: Plural nouns and derived nominals need POS/number/state before any verb-family reuse.
what_failed: A verb-entry family supplied a candidate for a nominal plural surface.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: كُفُّوٓا۟ is a finite command/verb-like surface in a noun tranche; exact POS and mood must be reviewed before hover trust
drill_prompt: Classify كُفُّوٓا۟ and list the sarf/nahw gates it needs.
learner_explanation: كُفُّوٓا۟ cannot be rich-certified from the noun entry label alone.
nahw_lesson: Mood/governance and clause context decide whether this is imperative/jussive-style wording.
sarf_lesson: Noun-tranche rows can still expose verb-form leakage and must not be certified from entry text alone.
what_failed: A noun entry candidate carried a verb-infinitive style hover onto a token needing exact POS/mood review.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: كُفُوًا may only need rich metadata if wording survives review; missing rendering alone is not an applyable hover repair
drill_prompt: Decide whether كُفُوًا needs wording repair or only rich renderer metadata.
learner_explanation: A correct-looking “equal” hover is not rich-certified until it has learner-visible grammar metadata.
nahw_lesson: If the syntactic role is not certified, keep the rich role pending instead of inventing a grammar label.
sarf_lesson: Renderer-only rows still need POS/case/state metadata, but they do not force a new lemma rule.
what_failed: A string-populated row lacked parse-key/display segments but did not prove a fresh wording defect.
